cases.prompt: app.py has a syntax error and will not compile. Fix it so it compiles cleanly. Keep the intended behaviour (a greet function that returns a greeting).
Implement the add(a, b) function in app.py so it returns the sum of a and b. The tests in test_app.py must pass. Do not edit the tests.

files.test_app.py:
from app import add

def test_add():
    assert add(2, 3) == 5
    assert add(-1, 1) == 0

cases.prompt: sum_to_n(n) should return the sum of integers from 1 to n inclusive, but it has a bug. Fix app.py so the tests pass. Do not edit the tests.

files.test_app.py:
from app import sum_to_n

def test_sum():
    assert sum_to_n(5) == 15
    assert sum_to_n(1) == 1
